scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with no existing <meta charset> and default html syntax creates a new meta charset element
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = Document.createShell("http://example.com"); // head exists with no meta charset; updateMetaCharset default false
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.updateMetaCharsetElement() == true; doc.outputSettings().charset() is set to provided Charset; doc.head().selectFirst("meta[charset]").attr("charset") equals provided Charset.displayName(); no elements matching "meta[name=charset]" remain

scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with existing <meta charset> and default html syntax updates existing element
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = Document.createShell("base"); doc.head().appendElement("meta").attr("charset","Old"); // simulate existing meta charset
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.updateMetaCharsetElement() == true; doc.outputSettings().charset() is set; doc.head().selectFirst("meta[charset]").attr("charset") equals provided Charset.displayName()
pseudocode: TEST TC02 charset updates existing meta

scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with xml syntax and no existing XmlDeclaration prepends a new xml declaration
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = new Document("http://u"); doc.updateMetaCharsetElement(false); doc.outputSettings().syntax(OutputSettings.Syntax.xml); // no XmlDeclaration child
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.updateMetaCharsetElement() == true; doc.outputSettings().charset() equals provided; first child is XmlDeclaration with name "xml", attr("encoding")==provided.displayName(), attr("version")=="1.0"
pseudocode: TEST TC03 charset xml creates declaration

scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with xml syntax and existing xml declaration updates attributes when name is xml
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = new Document("u"); XmlDeclaration decl = new XmlDeclaration("xml", false); decl.attr("version","2.0"); decl.attr("encoding","Old"); doc.prependChild(decl); doc.outputSettings().syntax(OutputSettings.Syntax.xml);
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.updateMetaCharsetElement() == true; doc.outputSettings().charset() equals provided; doc.childNodes().get(0) is same XmlDeclaration; its attr("encoding")==provided.displayName(), attr("version")=="1.0"
pseudocode: TEST TC04 charset xml updates declaration
